text: The subject property is located within the Bloomington–Normal metropolitan area, a stable Central Illinois market that serves as a regional center for employment, healthcare, education, and commerce. The local economy benefits from a diversified employment base, including healthcare, higher education, insurance, manufacturing, and public-sector employment, which contributes to consistent demand for neighborhood-oriented commercial real estate. Population levels in the Bloomington–Normal area have remained relatively stable, supporting steady consumer spending patterns and ongoing demand for local retail and service uses. Neighborhood and community-scale retail properties in Bloomington–Normal continue to be supported by established residential areas and daily consumer needs. Demand remains strongest for service-oriented tenants, food-related uses, personal services, and small professional offices, which are less susceptible to e-commerce disruption. While new retail development has been limited in recent years due to higher construction and financing costs, existing retail properties in established corridors benefit from reduced competitive pressure and stable occupancy levels. The subject’s location along Morrissey Drive places it within a recognized neighborhood commercial corridor characterized by similar small-scale retail and service uses.
